summary:
The ethmoid sinuses form earliest among paranasal sinuses starting from approximately nine weeks gestation; thus, these were likely present or nearly complete at birth before other sinuses like the maxillary formed later. Agent1 reasoned based on documented timelines showing initial stages of nasal cavity structures' growth indicating priority given to forming ethmoids prior to subsequent developments leading up to full birthing readiness where all major features should ideally have some degree of maturity if not entirely so for functional purposes immediately thereafter especially concerning respiratory health aspects pertinent here due their role within nasopharynx region facilitating drainage etc..

Agent1 reasoned based on documented timelines showing initial stages of nasal cavity structures' growth indicating priority given to forming ethmoids prior to subsequent developments leading up to full birthing readiness where all major features should ideally have some degree of maturity if not entirely so for functional purposes immediately thereafter especially concerning respiratory health aspects pertinent here due their role within nasopharynx region facilitating drainage etc..
